description: First-ever UN report focused on bodily autonomy, finding that nearly half of women in 57 developing countries cannot make decisions about their own healthcare, contraception, or sexual activity.

title: ILO Action Plan for Gender Equality 2022-2025
description: ILO action plan outlining gender-responsive strategies for decent work, aligning with the Centenary Declaration's gender-transformative agenda and addressing post-COVID gender-responsive recovery.

title: Sustainable Rural Livelihoods: A Framework for Analysis
description: Foundational IDS Working Paper 72 that established the Sustainable Livelihoods Framework, defining livelihoods in terms of access to natural, economic, human, and social capitals combined in pursuit of agricultural intensification, diversification, and migration strategies.

title: Sustainable Rural Livelihoods: Practical Concepts for the 21st Century
description: Seminal IDS Discussion Paper 296 that coined the term 'sustainable livelihoods' and introduced the capability-based framework linking livelihood resources, strategies, and vulnerability to poverty outcomes.

title: Voices of the Poor: Crying Out for Change
authors: Deepa Narayan, Robert Chambers, Meera K. Shah, Patti Petesch
description: Landmark World Bank participatory study drawing on the voices of over 60,000 poor people in 23 countries, documenting how the poor experience livelihood insecurity, social exclusion, and lack of assets, and calling for empowerment-centered development.

title: World Development Report 2013: Jobs
description: World Bank flagship report analyzing how jobs drive development by reducing poverty, empowering women, and building social cohesion, emphasizing that the private sector accounts for 90% of jobs in developing countries.

title: World Development Report 2008: Agriculture for Development
description: Influential World Bank report reassessing agriculture as a vital instrument for pro-poor economic development, examining how smallholder agriculture, value chains, and rural non-farm activities can reduce poverty.

title: Human Development Report 2015: Work for Human Development
description: UNDP global report arguing for a broader concept of work beyond employment, examining how work enhances human development through incomes, livelihoods, poverty reduction, dignity, and social participation.

title: Poor Economics: A Radical Rethinking of the Way to Fight Global Poverty
description: Nobel laureates' book based on 15 years of randomized experiments across developing countries, challenging conventional assumptions about poverty and showing how the poor make livelihood decisions about savings, entrepreneurship, health, and education.

title: Six Randomized Evaluations of Microcredit: Introduction and Further Steps
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Dean Karlan, Jonathan Zinman
description: Introduction to a landmark special issue summarizing six RCTs of microcredit across six countries on four continents, finding a consistent pattern of modestly positive but not transformative effects on borrowers' livelihoods.

title: Microcredit Impacts: Evidence from a Randomized Microcredit Program Placement Experiment by Compartamos Banco
authors: Manuela Angelucci, Dean Karlan, Jonathan Zinman
description: Clustered RCT evaluating microcredit expansion at 110% APR by Mexico's largest microlender, finding no evidence of transformative impacts across microenterprise, income, labor supply, expenditure, or well-being outcomes.

title: Estimating the Impact of Microcredit on Those Who Take It Up: Evidence from a Randomized Experiment in Morocco
authors: Bruno Crepon, Florencia Devoto, Esther Duflo, William Pariente
description: RCT of microcredit in rural Morocco showing that among likely borrowers, credit access led to significant increases in self-employment assets and profits but also reduced casual labor income, with no overall consumption gains.

title: The Impacts of Microfinance: Evidence from Joint-Liability Lending in Mongolia
authors: Orazio Attanasio, Britta Augsburg, Ralph De Haas, Emla Fitzsimons, Heike Harmgart
description: Randomized experiment in rural Mongolia finding that group lending increased female entrepreneurship and food consumption, while individual lending produced no significant poverty impacts, suggesting the two products serve complementary roles.

title: The Impacts of Microcredit: Evidence from Bosnia and Herzegovina
authors: Britta Augsburg, Ralph De Haas, Heike Harmgart, Costas Meghir
description: RCT of microcredit among marginal loan applicants in Bosnia, finding borrowers increased business investment and self-employment but also increased teenage labor supply while reducing school attendance, raising concerns about intergenerational effects.

title: Repayment Frequency and Default in Microfinance: Evidence from India
description: Field experiment in India randomly assigning microfinance clients to weekly or monthly repayment, finding no difference in default rates, implying MFIs can adopt more flexible schedules that reduce transaction costs without increasing risk.

title: Repayment Flexibility Can Reduce Financial Stress: A Randomized Control Trial with Microfinance Clients in India
authors: Erica Field, Rohini Pande, John Papp, Y. Jeanette Park
description: RCT in India finding that microfinance clients repaying monthly were 51% less likely to report financial anxiety, 54% more confident about repayment, and reported higher business investment and income compared to weekly repayment clients.

title: Sustainability of Microfinance Self Help Groups in India
description: World Bank Working Paper examining the SHG-bank linkage programme in India, analyzing SHG federations as an exit strategy for promoting organizations and assessing conditions for financial sustainability of the world's largest microfinance programme.

title: Economic and Social Impacts of Self-Help Groups in India
description: World Bank Working Paper using a large household survey to assess SHG impacts in India, finding positive effects on women's empowerment and nutritional intake, with empowerment benefits extending to non-participants through positive externalities.

title: Participation in Microfinance-Based Self Help Groups in India: Who Becomes a Member and for How Long?
description: Open-access study examining determinants of SHG membership using data from 15,300 women in Uttar Pradesh, finding that poorer households in the lowest quintiles were less likely to remain members when borrowing for enterprise purposes.

title: Rural Credit Programs and Women's Empowerment in Bangladesh
authors: Syed M. Hashemi, Sidney Ruth Schuler, Ann P. Riley
description: Influential study of Grameen Bank and BRAC showing that participation in credit programs is positively associated with eight dimensions of women's empowerment in rural Bangladesh, with effects on both participants and non-participants.

title: Savings Constraints and Microenterprise Development: Evidence from a Field Experiment in Kenya
description: Field experiment in Kenya providing savings accounts to market vendors, finding that women used accounts actively, increased savings and productive investment, implying significant barriers to savings constrain microenterprise growth.

title: Returns to Capital in Microenterprises: Evidence from a Field Experiment
authors: Suresh de Mel, David McKenzie, Christopher Woodruff
description: Randomized experiment providing cash and equipment grants to microenterprises in Sri Lanka, finding average real returns of 4.6-5.3% per month, substantially above market interest rates, but with significant gender heterogeneity showing no positive returns for female-owned enterprises.

title: Microentrepreneurship in Developing Countries
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Emily Breza, Esther Duflo, Cynthia Kinnan
description: NBER Working Paper reviewing the state of knowledge on microentrepreneurship in developing countries, concluding that microcredit helps some enterprises but is not a silver bullet for transforming most informal firms into fast-growing businesses.

title: Teaching Entrepreneurship: Impact of Business Training on Microfinance Clients and Institutions
description: First RCT evaluating business training for microfinance clients, conducted with FINCA in Peru, finding improved business knowledge and MFI client retention but negligible effects on revenue, profits, or employment among female microentrepreneurs.

title: Why Do People Stay Poor?
authors: Clare Balboni, Oriana Bandiera, Robin Burgess, Maitreesh Ghatak, Anton Heil
description: Study using BRAC's TUP programme data over 11 years in Bangladesh to test between fundamentals vs. poverty traps views, finding strong evidence for poverty traps with a clear asset threshold below which households cannot escape poverty without large transfers.

title: From Extreme Poverty to Sustainable Livelihoods: A Technical Guide to the Graduation Approach
description: Practical technical guide for implementing the graduation model pioneered by BRAC, combining consumption support, asset transfers, skills training, coaching, and savings access to help ultra-poor households build sustainable livelihoods.

title: Graduating the Poor: Completion Report of CGAP's Graduating the Poor Initiative
description: Completion report finding that graduation programs are more cost-effective than lump-sum cash transfers or standalone livelihoods programs for the extreme poor, with nearly 60 actors now testing or scaling the approach worldwide.

title: Graduation Pathways: Increasing Income and Resilience for the Extreme Poor
description: CGAP brief describing how the time-bound graduation approach sequences social assistance, skills training, seed capital, financial education, savings access, and mentoring over 24-26 months to move households out of extreme poverty.

title: Crafting a Graduation Pathway for the Ultra Poor: Lessons and Evidence from a BRAC Programme
authors: Imran Matin, Munshi Sulaiman, Mehnaz Rabbani
description: Early analysis of BRAC's Targeting the Ultra Poor programme documenting the graduation model's design, implementation, and initial evidence of outcomes in Bangladesh, including asset accumulation and livelihood diversification.

title: Vocational and Skills Training Programs to Improve Labor Market Outcomes
description: J-PAL policy review of 28 studies on vocational training across developing countries, finding that programs combining classroom training with practical experience and soft skills were most effective at improving employment and earnings.

title: Generating Skilled Self-Employment in Developing Countries: Experimental Evidence from Uganda
authors: Christopher Blattman, Nathan Fiala, Sebastian Martinez
description: RCT of Uganda's Youth Opportunities Program providing cash grants for vocational training and business start-up, finding 57% increase in business assets, 17% increase in work hours, and 38% earnings gain after four years, with especially large impacts for women.

title: Safe Spaces with Vocational and Life Skills Training for Young Women's Economic and Social Empowerment in Uganda
authors: Oriana Bandiera, Niklas Buehren, Robin Burgess, Markus Goldstein, Selim Gulesci, Imran Rasul, Munshi Sulaiman
description: Evaluation of BRAC's ELA program in Uganda combining vocational and life skills training for adolescent girls, finding substantial advances in economic empowerment and bodily autonomy that persisted for four years.

title: Making Labor Markets Work for the Youth: An Approach Paper
description: World Bank approach paper noting that 1.1 billion of the world's 1.2 billion youth live in the Global South, proposing a policy framework combining vocational training, mentoring, entrepreneurship support, and access to finance for youth employment.

title: Youth Employment in Sub-Saharan Africa
description: World Bank Africa Development Series book analyzing youth employment dynamics including the demographic transition, untapped farming opportunities, and youth aspirations in Sub-Saharan Africa, with policy recommendations for improving livelihoods.

title: Youth Aspirations and the Reality of Jobs in Developing Countries: Mind the Gap
description: OECD study drawing on school-to-work transition surveys in 32 developing countries, analyzing the gap between youth career aspirations and available job quality, and proposing policies to improve youth employment outcomes.

title: Economics and Emigration: Trillion-Dollar Bills on the Sidewalk?
description: CGD Working Paper demonstrating that barriers to labor mobility from poor countries represent the largest class of distortions in the global economy, with estimated gains from eliminating them ranging from 50-150% of world GDP.

title: International Migration, Remittances, and Household Investment: Evidence from Philippine Migrants' Exchange Rate Shocks
description: Study exploiting the 1997 Asian financial crisis to show that positive exchange rate shocks to Philippine migrants lead to increased remittances, higher child schooling, reduced child labor, and greater household entrepreneurship in origin communities.

title: Workers' Remittances: An Important and Stable Source of External Development Finance
description: Pioneering World Bank analysis that brought remittances to global attention, showing they are larger than official aid, counter-cyclical to recipient economies, and represent the second-largest source of external finance for developing countries.

title: Remittances and Poverty in Migrants' Home Areas: Evidence from the Philippines
description: Study using exchange rate shocks as natural experiments to show that favorable shocks lead to remittance increases that reduce aggregate poverty rates in Philippine migrant-origin communities.

title: Migration and Development Brief 39
description: World Bank/KNOMAD brief reporting that remittance flows to LMICs reached $656 billion in 2023, surpassing FDI and ODA, while documenting that global remittance costs remain at 6.2%, more than twice the SDG target of 3%.

title: Investing in Smallholder Agriculture for Food Security
authors: HLPE (High Level Panel of Experts on Food Security and Nutrition)
description: FAO HLPE Report examining smallholder agriculture's contribution to food security across availability, access, utilization, and stability dimensions, noting that 70% of the world's 1.4 billion extremely poor depend partly or completely on agriculture.

title: Smallholders, Food Security, and the Environment
description: IFAD-UNEP report on smallholders' critical role in food security and environmental sustainability, recommending conservation agriculture and mixed crop-livestock-agroforestry systems to enhance rural livelihoods while protecting biodiversity.

title: The Economic Lives of Smallholder Farmers
description: FAO analytical report using the Smallholder Farmers' DataPortrait to examine economic conditions and livelihood strategies of smallholders across multiple countries, documenting the diversity of income sources and asset levels.

title: Rural Development Report 2021: Transforming Food Systems for Rural Prosperity
description: IFAD flagship report finding that 3 billion people live on 500 million small-scale farms in LMICs, producing 30-34% of global food supply, and arguing food systems must be reshaped to provide decent livelihoods for all who grow, process, and market food.

title: Rural Development Report 2019: Creating Opportunities for Rural Youth
description: IFAD report focused on the nearly 1 billion rural youth in developing countries, analyzing how agricultural transformation, rural non-farm activities, and migration create livelihood opportunities for young people.

title: Linking African Smallholders to High-Value Markets
authors: Spencer Henson, Steven Jaffee, John Cranfield, Jose Blandon, Paul Siegel
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper analyzing how smallholder access to high-value agricultural markets can diversify livelihoods and reduce rural poverty, examining constraints from farm-level resources, logistics, and transaction costs.

title: Working with Smallholders: A Handbook for Firms Building Sustainable Supply Chains
description: IFC/World Bank handbook guiding firms on expanding supply chains by working with smallholder farmers, covering approaches to improve productivity, farming practices, and livelihoods through value chain integration in emerging markets.

title: Value Chain Microfinance
description: NBER Working Paper studying how provision of financial services within value chain relationships can overcome barriers to financial access for small firms and workers in developing countries, reviewing empirical literature at the intersection of value chains and microfinance.

title: The Impact of Ethiopia's Productive Safety Net Programme and its Linkages
authors: Daniel Gilligan, John Hoddinott, Alemayehu Seyoum Taffesse
description: IFPRI evaluation of Sub-Saharan Africa's largest social protection program, finding that while the PSNP alone had limited impact due to below-target transfers, combining it with agricultural support packages significantly improved food security and livelihood outcomes.

title: Long-Term Impacts of Conditional Cash Transfers: Review of the Evidence
authors: Sarah Baird, Francisco H.G. Ferreira, Berk Ozler, Michael Woolcock
description: World Bank Research Observer review of CCT long-term evidence highlighting that while short-term impacts on schooling and health are well-established, long-term effects on livelihoods and adult earnings remain mixed and methodologically challenging to measure.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Conditional Cash Transfer Programs
description: World Bank analysis of CCT evaluations across Colombia, Honduras, Jamaica, Mexico, Nicaragua, and Turkey, finding clear evidence of success in increasing enrollment, improving preventive health care, and raising household consumption.

title: Enhancing Productivity in the Urban Informal Economy
authors: WIEGO (Women in Informal Employment: Globalizing and Organizing)
description: WIEGO research report on how cities can support informal workers' livelihoods by providing infrastructure, enabling access to public space, and recognizing the contribution of informal enterprises to urban economies.

title: The Informal Economy and Decent Work: A Policy Resource Guide
description: Comprehensive ILO guide presenting strategies for transitioning informal workers to decent work across policy areas including employment, social protection, rights, regulatory frameworks, and equality, recognizing that millions pursue livelihoods in conditions of informality.

title: The Urban Informal Economy: Achieving Prospering and Territorial Equality
description: GOLD VI Working Paper documenting that informal employment predominates in urban areas of the Global South, analyzing how urban governance and planning can better support the livelihoods of informal workers and enterprises.

title: Informal Economy and the World Bank
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper examining the Bank's approach to the informal sector, analyzing how informality affects livelihoods and labor market outcomes across developing countries.

title: World Employment and Social Outlook: Trends 2024
description: ILO flagship report analyzing global labor market trends, finding that the jobs gap reached 402 million people in 2024, with persistent gender gaps, high youth unemployment at 12.6%, and informal work returning to pre-pandemic levels.

title: Can Social Enterprises Aid Sustainable Development? Evidence from Multi-Stage Investigations
authors: Divya Aggarwal, Varun Elembilassery, R. Sujatha
description: Open-access study examining social enterprises' role in sustainable development in India, finding growth in healthcare, agriculture, education, energy, affordable housing, livelihoods promotion, and financial inclusion sectors.

title: The Rural Non-Farm Economy, Livelihoods and Their Diversification: Issues and Options
description: Working paper analyzing how rural populations across Africa, Asia, and Latin America derive 30-50% of income from non-farm activities, examining 55 donor projects and summarizing policy options for supporting livelihood diversification.

title: Growing the Rural Non-Farm Economy to Alleviate Poverty: An IEG Evaluation of World Bank Group Support 2004-2014
description: IEG evaluation of $46.5 billion in World Bank support for rural non-farm activities, finding that poverty-aimed interventions reduced vulnerability but did not generate sufficient sustained income to lift large numbers out of poverty.

title: A Sustainable Livelihoods Framework for the 21st Century
authors: Nitya Natarajan, Andrew Newsham, Jonathan Rigg, Diana Suhardiman
description: World Development paper proposing a reformulation of the classic Sustainable Livelihoods Framework, addressing critiques of theory, method, scale, politics, and decolonizing knowledge while updating the framework for contemporary development challenges.

title: Using Randomization in Development Economics Research: A Toolkit
authors: Esther Duflo, Rachel Glennerster, Michael Kremer
description: Foundational methodological toolkit for designing and conducting randomized evaluations in development economics, covering practical guidance on randomization techniques, sample size, and threats to validity.

title: Impact Evaluation in Practice, Second Edition
authors: Paul J. Gertler, Sebastian Martinez, Patrick Premand, Laura B. Rawlings, Christel M. J. Vermeersch
description: Comprehensive World Bank handbook covering the full range of impact evaluation methods including randomized evaluations, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, difference-in-differences, and matching, with practical examples from development programmes.

title: Handbook on Impact Evaluation: Quantitative Methods and Practices
authors: Shahidur R. Khandker, Gayatri B. Koolwal, Hussain A. Samad
description: World Bank handbook providing detailed guidance on quantitative impact evaluation methods including propensity score matching, difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity design for assessing development programme effectiveness.

title: Understanding and Misunderstanding Randomized Controlled Trials
description: Critical assessment of the use of RCTs in social science and development, arguing that RCTs do not occupy a special position in a hierarchy of evidence and that their results require the same careful interpretation as other methods.

title: Instruments, Randomization, and Learning about Development
description: Influential critique published in the Journal of Economic Literature examining the limitations of instrumental variable techniques and RCTs in development economics, arguing for the importance of understanding causal mechanisms beyond project-level evaluations.

title: Randomized Control Trials in the Field of Development: A Critical Perspective
authors: Florent Bédécarrats, Isabelle Guérin, François Roubaud (Eds.)
description: Open-access edited volume providing a comprehensive critical assessment of RCTs in development, examining their strengths, weaknesses, and the conditions under which they succeed or fail, with contributions from leading evaluation specialists.

title: Evaluation in the Practice of Development
description: World Bank Research Observer article proposing ten steps for making impact evaluations more relevant to development practitioners, arguing that standard evaluation methods leave significant gaps between what we know and what we need to know about development effectiveness.

title: Theory-Based Impact Evaluation: Principles and Practice
description: 3ie working paper establishing six principles for successful theory-based impact evaluation: mapping the causal chain, understanding context, anticipating heterogeneity, using credible counterfactuals, rigorous factual analysis, and employing mixed methods.

title: Broadening the Range of Designs and Methods for Impact Evaluations
authors: Elliot Stern, Nicoletta Stame, John Mayne, Kim Forss, Rick Davies, Barbara Befani
description: DFID Working Paper 38 arguing for expanding impact evaluation beyond experimental methods to include theory-based, case-based, and participatory approaches, providing a framework for evaluating complex development interventions.

title: Models of Causality and Causal Inference
description: Annex to DFID's broadening impact evaluation study, providing an accessible overview of three major models of causality used in evaluation: Mill's methods, generative/mechanism-based causality, and configurational causality.

title: Impact Evaluations and Development: NONIE Guidance on Impact Evaluation
description: Guidance from the Network of Networks for Impact Evaluation providing step-by-step advice on planning and managing impact evaluations, stressing that no single method is best and that rigorous IE requires addressing core conceptual and methodological issues.

title: Using Case Studies to Explore the External Validity of Complex Development Interventions
description: Argues that for complex development interventions, external validity should be assumed to be zero by default, and proposes deploying case studies to identify the conditions under which diverse outcomes are observed across contexts.

title: Quasi-Experimental Design and Methods
authors: Patricia Rogers, Irene Guijt (UNICEF Methodological Briefs: Impact Evaluation No. 8)
description: UNICEF methodological brief providing accessible guidance on quasi-experimental evaluation methods including propensity score matching, regression discontinuity, and difference-in-differences for evaluating development programmes when randomization is not feasible.

title: Theory of Change (Methodological Briefs: Impact Evaluation No. 2)
description: UNICEF methodological brief explaining how to develop and use theories of change in impact evaluation, covering situation analysis, causal pathways, assumptions testing, and the role of theory in evaluation design.

title: Theory of Change (Monitoring and Evaluation Series Paper 16)
description: Practical guide tracing the origins and evolution of theory of change since the 1990s, explaining how to develop and use theories of change for programme planning, monitoring, and evaluation in development contexts.

title: Applying a Theory of Change Approach to the Evaluation of Comprehensive Community Initiatives
description: Aspen Institute Roundtable paper advocating for theory of change as a framework for evaluating complex community initiatives, demonstrating how it sharpens planning, specifies intended outcomes, and strengthens evaluation design.

title: Evaluating the Logical Framework Approach: Towards Learning-Oriented Development Evaluation
description: Critical assessment of the logical framework approach in development evaluation, identifying recurrent failings and arguing that logframes' focus on intended effects makes them limiting for evaluation, especially at the expense of learning.

title: The Logical Framework (M&E Universe Paper)
description: Practical guide on the logical framework as a planning, monitoring, and evaluation tool, tracing its history from military planning and NASA to adoption by USAID and spread throughout the development sector.

title: Outcome Mapping: Building Learning and Reflection into Development Programs
authors: Sarah Earl, Fred Carden, Terry Smutylo
description: IDRC book presenting a participatory M&E methodology that focuses on behavioural changes in boundary partners rather than end-state outcomes, providing a practical approach to monitoring contributions to development change.

title: The Most Significant Change (MSC) Technique: A Guide to Its Use
description: Definitive guide to the MSC participatory monitoring technique developed in Bangladesh in the 1990s, involving systematic collection and selection of stories of significant change to monitor complex development programmes without quantitative indicators.

title: Contribution Analysis: An Approach to Exploring Cause and Effect
description: ILAC Brief No. 16 introducing contribution analysis as a practical approach for making credible causal claims about programme contributions to outcomes when experimental designs are not feasible, by verifying theories of change and examining alternative explanations.

title: Contribution Analysis: Coming of Age?
description: Landmark article tracing the history and conceptual underpinnings of contribution analysis, linking it to philosophical discussions on contributory causes, and demonstrating how the approach has matured as a methodology for evaluating complex interventions.

title: Realist Evaluation
description: Overview paper outlining the realist evaluation approach which begins and ends with theory, developing and testing context-mechanism-outcome configurations to understand what works for whom, in what contexts, and why.

title: Realistic Evaluation: An Overview
description: Accessible overview of the realistic evaluation framework developed by Pawson and Tilley, explaining context-mechanism-outcome (CMO) configurations and how they can be tested empirically to inform policy and practice improvement.

title: OECD DAC Evaluation Criteria: Adapted Definitions and Principles for Use
description: Official DAC document updating the six evaluation criteria (relevance, coherence, effectiveness, efficiency, impact, sustainability) used globally to evaluate development cooperation, reflecting the 2030 Agenda and Paris Agreement.

title: Applying Evaluation Criteria Thoughtfully
description: Practical guidance supporting evaluators and commissioners in the thoughtful application of the OECD DAC evaluation criteria, to be used alongside the DAC Quality Standards for Development Evaluation.

title: Results-Based Management Handbook: Harmonizing RBM Concepts and Approaches for Improved Development Results at Country Level
description: UN system handbook harmonizing results-based management concepts, providing guidance on defining results, formulating indicators, and linking RBM to the United Nations Development Assistance Framework.

title: Results Based Management in the Development Co-operation Agencies: A Review of Experience
description: OECD background document reviewing how development agencies have introduced results-based management systems, identifying common challenges and lessons learned from early RBM implementation across bilateral and multilateral organizations.

title: Measuring and Managing Results in Development Co-operation: A Review of Challenges and Practices among DAC Members and Observers
description: Comprehensive OECD review examining how results information is used for accountability and management by DAC members, identifying good practices and challenges in measuring and managing development results.

title: Handbook on Planning, Monitoring and Evaluating for Development Results
description: UNDP's comprehensive handbook for programme managers providing practical tools and guidance for results-oriented planning, monitoring, and evaluation of development programmes.

title: UNDP Evaluation Guidelines
description: Updated guidelines reflecting changes in UNDP's evaluation policy, providing greater detail on expected roles and responsibilities, and including links to examples of good quality evaluations across the organization.

title: USAID Evaluation Policy
authors: United States Agency for International Development
description: Updated USAID evaluation policy providing clarity on purposes of evaluation, types of evaluations required and recommended, and approaches for conducting, disseminating, and using evaluations in development programming.

title: USAID Agency Learning Agenda FY 2022-2026
authors: United States Agency for International Development
description: USAID's strategic learning agenda identifying priority research and evaluation questions, demonstrating the agency's commitment to evidence-based decision making and becoming a learning organization.

title: DFID Evaluation Strategy
description: DFID's strategic framework for evaluation outlining four priorities: enhancing monitoring, generating knowledge on MEL for adaptive management, investing in rigorous evaluations, and using diverse evidence to inform decisions.

title: DFID's Approach to Value for Money in Programme and Portfolio Management
description: Independent review assessing DFID's approach to value for money using the 4Es framework (economy, efficiency, effectiveness, equity), finding that DFID pioneered VfM assessment among international donors but identifying areas for improvement.

title: Integrating Value for Money and Impact Evaluations: Issues, Institutions, and Opportunities
authors: Jyotsna Puri, Anastasia Aladysheva, Vegard Iversen, Yanyan Ghorpade, Tomoko Brück
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper exploring how to integrate value for money analysis with impact evaluation practice, reviewing VfM field methods and proposing approaches for combining cost and effectiveness evidence.

title: Participatory Evaluation (M&E Universe Paper)
description: Practical guide on participatory evaluation approaches in development, covering methods for collecting and analysing information with programme participants and stakeholders, including Participatory Learning and Action (PLA).

title: Introduction to Mixed Methods in Impact Evaluation
authors: InterAction with support from the Rockefeller Foundation
description: Guidance note explaining why mixed methods matter in impact evaluation, covering design applications, managing mixed methods evaluations, and case studies demonstrating how combining quantitative and qualitative approaches strengthens evaluation findings.

title: Adaptive Management (M&E Universe Paper)
description: Briefing paper on adaptive management in development, explaining how organisations can use structured iterative approaches to manage complex programmes, integrating learning and evidence into ongoing decision-making.

title: Monitoring and Evaluation for Adaptive Management Working Paper 2: What Is Adaptive Management and How Does It Work?
description: Working paper from the Global Learning for Adaptive Management (GLAM) initiative defining adaptive management and examining how M&E systems can support adaptive programming in complex development contexts.

title: Complexity-Aware Methods (Fact Sheet)
description: USAID/MEASURE Evaluation fact sheet on complexity-aware evaluation methods that address the inherent complexity of modern development programs operating in dynamic environments with multiple stakeholders and sectors.

title: 3ie Evidence Gap Maps: A Starting Point for Strategic Evidence Production and Use
authors: Birte Snilstveit, Radhika Bhatia, Kristen Rankin, Beryl Leach
description: 3ie Working Paper 28 describing the evidence gap map methodology pioneered by 3ie, which provides visual overviews of existing impact evaluations and systematic reviews in a development sector to identify evidence gaps and inform strategic research priorities.

title: Evidence and Gap Maps: A Comparison of Different Approaches
description: Open-access comparison of different evidence mapping approaches used in international development, examining how 3ie's EGM methodology relates to scoping reviews and other evidence synthesis methods, and assessing methodological differences in search, inclusion criteria, and quality appraisal.

title: UN Women Evaluation Handbook: How to Manage Gender-Responsive Evaluations
description: Comprehensive handbook for managing gender-responsive evaluations, incorporating principles of gender equality and women's rights into evaluation approaches, methods, processes, and use of findings.

title: Good Practices in Gender-Responsive Evaluations
description: Knowledge product identifying trends and good practices in how UNEG partners have integrated gender-responsive approaches and methods in evaluation processes to advance gender equality and women's empowerment.

title: Gender-Responsive Evaluation (M&E Universe Paper)
description: Practical briefing paper on gender-responsive evaluation covering how to assess the degree to which gender and power relationships change as a result of an intervention and ensure women's voices are prominent throughout the evaluation process.

title: Integrating Gender Equality in Monitoring and Evaluation
description: ILO guidance note explaining why and how to integrate gender equality systematically into monitoring and evaluation processes, producing evaluations that contribute to more effective, inclusive, and sustainable development outcomes.

title: The Government Monitoring and Evaluation System in India: A Work in Progress
description: World Bank IEG working paper examining the evolution of India's M&E system, covering outcome budgeting by the Ministry of Finance, the Independent Evaluation Office under the Planning Commission, and the Performance Management and Evaluation System under the Cabinet Secretariat.

title: Monitoring, Evaluation and Performance Management in South Asia: The Challenge of Building Capacity
description: Reviews the state of M&E systems across South Asia, examining tools, budgets, and institutional arrangements for evaluation, and makes the case for building M&E capacity to improve accountability for government spending in the region.

title: Evaluation Capacity Development: Building Country Capacity for Monitoring and Evaluation in the Public Sector
description: World Bank OED publication drawing on international experience to provide lessons on building country-level M&E capacity, covering performance evaluation systems, demand and supply factors, and strategies for institutionalising evaluation in the public sector.

title: Evaluation Capacity Development in Asia: Selected Proceedings from the International Conference
description: Proceedings exploring evaluation capacity in Asian countries including India, China, and others, examining the interface between results-based management and M&E, and strategies for building evaluation demand and supply.

title: Beneficiary Feedback Mechanisms (M&E Universe Paper)
description: Practical guide on establishing beneficiary feedback mechanisms in development programmes, covering tools such as suggestion boxes, surveys, and mobile-based methods, and how to build trust through responsive feedback loops.

title: Improving Beneficiary Feedback Mechanisms for Refugees
description: DFID-funded review examining best practices, impacts, and digital technology use in beneficiary feedback mechanisms for refugee programming, finding that sustained organisational impact from feedback remains limited.

title: Data Quality for Monitoring and Evaluation Systems (Fact Sheet)
description: USAID-funded fact sheet on ensuring data quality in M&E systems, covering data quality assessment tools, capabilities, and approaches for documenting and improving the quality of monitoring data used for programme decisions.

title: Introduction to Cost-Effectiveness Analysis
authors: Development Monitoring and Evaluation Office (DMEO), NITI Aayog, Government of India
description: India's DMEO guide introducing cost-effectiveness analysis for evaluating development programmes, produced under the CLEAR South Asia initiative in partnership with J-PAL, tailored for the Indian evaluation context.

title: Impact Evaluability Toolkit
authors: J-PAL South Asia, CLEAR South Asia
description: Framework developed by J-PAL and CLEAR South Asia for assessing when and how to conduct counterfactual impact evaluations of government programmes in India, providing practical steps for selecting evaluation candidates and choosing appropriate methods.

title: Outcome Mapping (Knowledge Solutions)
description: ADB knowledge solutions brief summarising outcome mapping as an evaluation approach for development programmes, focusing on monitoring changes in the behaviour of boundary partners rather than measuring end-state development impacts.

title: Challenges and Opportunities for Universal Health Coverage in South Asia: A Scoping Review
authors: Jayendra Sharma, Milena Pavlova, Wim Groot
description: Scoping review examining challenges and opportunities for UHC across South Asian countries, identifying underfunding, fragmented health systems, and inadequate insurance coverage as key barriers.

title: Advancing Universal Health Coverage in the WHO South-East Asia Region with a Focus on Human Resources for Health
description: Examines progress toward UHC in the WHO South-East Asia Region, highlighting the UHC service coverage index improvement from 47 in 2010 to 62 in 2021, with a focus on human resources for health.

title: Extending Universal Health Coverage to Informal Workers: A Systematic Review of Health Financing Schemes in Low- and Middle-Income Countries in Southeast Asia
authors: Nattanicha Wattanapisit, Wallaporn Srisakdakorn, Viroj Tangcharoensathien
description: Systematic review of health financing schemes implemented to extend UHC to informal workers in Southeast Asia, a region characterized by high prevalence of informal employment.

title: Moving toward Universal Health Coverage with a National Health Insurance Program: A Scoping Review of Experiences in Eleven Low- and Lower-Middle-Income Countries
authors: Katya Galactionova, Elizabeth Kalesha, Abena Asumadu-Sakyi, Fabrizio Tediosi
description: Assesses strategies adopted by low- and lower-middle-income countries with a national health insurance scheme to expand UHC, examining variations in progress across eleven countries.

title: Achieving Universal Health Coverage in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: Challenges for Policy Post-Pandemic and Beyond
authors: Oyeli Ochie, Olalekan Olasehinde, Rebecca Adegboyega
description: Narrative review of 118 peer-reviewed and grey literature sources examining UHC challenges in LMICs, including weak health systems, financing gaps, and epidemiological transitions exacerbated by COVID-19.

title: Tracking Universal Health Coverage: 2023 Global Monitoring Report
description: Joint WHO-World Bank global monitoring report tracking progress toward UHC, concluding the world is off track for significant progress by 2030 with persistent gaps in service coverage and financial protection.

title: Strengthening Primary Health Care Through Community Health Workers in South Asia
authors: Shyam Sundar Budhathoki, Samiksha Singh, Basnama Ayaz, Zulfiqar A Bhutta
description: Comparative analysis of community health worker programs in Bangladesh, India, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka, examining successes, challenges, and reforms for better-performing CHW programs.

title: Workload and Emerging Challenges of Community Health Workers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Mixed-Methods Systematic Review
authors: Tegbar Yigzaw, Wondwosen Shiferaw, Frehiwot Berhane, Mengistu Hailemariam
description: Systematic review summarizing community health workers' perceived workload in LMICs, finding that excessive workload affects productivity and retention of CHWs in remote and underserved areas.

title: Community Health Workers and COVID-19: Cross-Country Evidence on Their Roles, Experiences, Challenges and Adaptive Strategies
authors: Meenakshi Gautham, Kok Maryse, Kerry Scott, Shivam Gupta, Maryse C Kok
description: Cross-country study examining CHWs' contributions during COVID-19 in India, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Sierra Leone, Kenya, and Ethiopia, analyzing their frontline roles, challenges, and adaptive strategies.

title: Task Shifting Healthcare Services in the Post-COVID World: A Scoping Review
authors: Emily Liang, Arjun Mitra, Sonia Abi Jaoude, Veronica Vargas
description: Scoping review of task shifting and task sharing approaches that move tasks from highly trained professionals to other providers, examining evidence and implications for health service delivery in LMICs.

title: Task Sharing with Non-Physician Health-Care Workers for Management of Blood Pressure in Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
authors: Tazeen H Jafar, Greet P Gandhi, Angel CY Ang, Mente Andrew
description: Systematic review and meta-analysis demonstrating the effectiveness of task sharing with non-physician health workers for blood pressure management in LMICs, finding that nurses and pharmacists were relatively more effective.

title: The Nutrition Sensitivity of Food and Agriculture in South Asia
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper reviewing evidence on the nutrition sensitivity of food and agriculture across eight South Asian countries, addressing the region's high burdens of child stunting and wasting.

title: The State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World 2023
description: Joint UN report providing global and regional estimates on hunger, food insecurity, and malnutrition, examining urbanization and agrifood system transformation as pathways to affordable healthy diets.

title: Regional Overview of Food Security and Nutrition 2020: Asia and the Pacific
description: Joint UN report on food security and nutrition in Asia and the Pacific, assessing progress toward SDG 2 targets and noting the expected impact of COVID-19 disruptions on all forms of malnutrition.

title: Factors Associated with Wasting Among Children Under Five Years Old in South Asia: Implications for Action
authors: Nita Dalmiya, Harriet Torlesse, Victor Aguayo
description: Analysis of factors associated with child wasting across South Asia finding that low maternal BMI is a significant predictor and boys are more likely to be wasted than girls in Afghanistan, Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan.

title: Revisiting Maternal and Child Undernutrition in Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries: Variable Progress Towards an Unfinished Agenda
authors: Robert E Black, Cesar G Victora, Susan P Walker, Zulfiqar A Bhutta, Parul Christian, Mercedes de Onis, Majid Ezzati, Sally Grantham-McGregor, Joanne Katz, Reynaldo Martorell, Ricardo Uauy
description: Lancet Series paper reviewing progress 13 years after the first Series on maternal and child undernutrition, finding that stunting prevalence has fallen but wasting and stunting remain major public health problems in low-income countries.

title: Evidence-Based Interventions for Improvement of Maternal and Child Nutrition: What Can Be Done and at What Cost?
authors: Zulfiqar A Bhutta, Jai K Das, Arjumand Rizvi, Michelle F Gaffey, Neff Walker, Susan Horton, Patrick Webb, Anna Lartey, Robert E Black
description: Lancet Nutrition Series paper providing systematic review of evidence-based nutrition interventions for maternal and child health, estimating costs and potential impact of scaling up in high-burden countries.

title: Contribution of Malnutrition to Infant and Child Deaths in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia
authors: Cynthia Boschi-Pinto, Emily R Smith, Jonathon L Simon, Myo Myo Mon, Alka Dev
description: Study using postmortem data from seven LMICs revealing that malnutrition plays a significant role in the causal chain of 40% of deaths among children under 5 years of age in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia.

title: Socioeconomic Inequalities in Access to Maternal Healthcare in South-Asian Countries: A Systematic Review
authors: Parveen Ali, Rubab Zahra, Shehla Naeem, Sana Waqas
description: Systematic review of 55 studies from India, Nepal, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, and Pakistan showing that maternal healthcare access is linked to economic status, education, and women's autonomy in patriarchal family structures.

title: Reproductive, Maternal, Newborn, and Child Health Intervention Coverage in 70 Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries, 2000-30: Trends, Projections, and Inequities
description: Lancet Global Health analysis projecting RMNCH intervention coverage across 70 LMICs through 2030, finding that only 18 countries will reach the 80% Composite Coverage Index target, with South Asia projected to reach 89.2%.

title: A Global Analysis of the Determinants of Maternal Health and Transitions in Maternal Mortality
description: Global Burden of Disease analysis examining determinants of maternal health transitions and maternal mortality trends, highlighting socioeconomic, health system, and demographic factors driving inequities.

title: Can Available Interventions End Preventable Deaths in Mothers, Newborn Babies, and Stillbirths, and at What Cost?
authors: Zulfiqar A Bhutta, Jai K Das, Rajiv Bahl, Joy E Lawn, Rehana A Salam, Vinod K Paul, M Jeeva Sankar, Hannah Blencowe, Arjumand Rizvi, Victoria B Chou, Neff Walker
description: Lancet Every Newborn Series paper modeling the effect and cost of scaling up interventions in 75 high-burden Countdown countries, finding that accelerated scale-up could prevent most newborn deaths at $1,928 per life saved.

title: Impact of mHealth Interventions on Maternal, Newborn, and Child Health from Conception to 24 Months Postpartum in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Systematic Review
authors: Anam Shahil Feroz, Naureen Akber Ali, Nadia Fareed, Sarah Saleem
description: Systematic review of mHealth technologies and their effectiveness on maternal, newborn, and child health outcomes during the first 1000 days of life in LMICs.

title: The Lancet Commission on Global Mental Health and Sustainable Development
authors: Vikram Patel, Shekhar Saxena, Crick Lund, Graham Thornicroft, Florence Baingana, Paul Bolton, Dan Chisholm, Pamela Y Collins, Janice L Cooper, Julian Eaton, Helen Herrman
description: Landmark Lancet Commission reassessing the global mental health agenda within the 2030 Sustainable Development framework, calling for closing gaps in access, quality, and prevention of mental health care worldwide.

title: COVID-19 and Mental Health in 8 Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Prospective Cohort Study
authors: Tassew Woldehanna, Anja Benshaul-Tolonen, Manuela Angelucci, Sandra Favara, Marta Favara
description: Prospective cohort study of 21,162 individuals across 8 LMICs in Asia, Africa, and South America, finding that depression symptoms increased significantly (0.29 SD) in the first 4 months following COVID-19 onset.

title: COVID-19 Mental Health Impact and Responses in Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries: Reimagining Global Mental Health
authors: Brandon A Kohrt, Emily Haroz, Judith K Bass, Adam Brown, Gloria A Pedersen, Mark J D Jordans, Wietse A Tol
description: Lancet Psychiatry analysis of how LMICs, historically receiving a small fraction of global mental health resources, rapidly developed COVID-19 mental health and psychosocial support response plans.

title: Mental Health Policy Implementation in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Realist Review Protocol
authors: Ruth Trainor, Florence Baingana, Assegid Debebe
description: Protocol for a realist synthesis explaining how, why, for whom, and under what health system conditions certain mental health policy implementation strategies work or fail in LMICs.

title: Data Gaps in Prevalence Rates of Mental Health Conditions Around the World: A Retrospective Analysis of Nationally Representative Data
authors: Lena C Quilty, Michael H Boyle, John Cairney, Guilherme Borges
description: Lancet Global Health analysis revealing substantial gaps in mental health prevalence data, particularly among children and in low-income countries, despite increased scientific production.

title: Fragmented Health Systems in COVID-19: Rectifying the Misalignment Between Global Health Security and Universal Health Coverage
authors: Abiodun Awosusi, Irene Akua Agyepong, Ifeanyi M Nsofor, Agnes Soucat
description: Lancet paper examining how COVID-19 exposed overconcentration on global health security at the expense of sustainable UHC investments, using examples from African countries.

title: Redesigning Health Systems for Global Health Security
authors: Irene Akua Agyepong, Uche V Amazigo, Gorik Ooms, Moffat J Nyirenda, Agnes Soucat
description: Lancet Global Health paper arguing for identifying and strengthening critical characteristics of resilient health systems, emphasizing health information systems and data platforms for effective epidemic detection.

title: Global, Regional, and National Health-Care Inefficiency and Associated Factors in 201 Countries, 1995-2022
description: Lancet Global Health study measuring health spending inefficiency across 201 countries, finding that expanding government health coverage and strengthening infrastructure could decrease health system inefficiency.

title: Measures to Strengthen Primary Health-Care Systems in Low- and Middle-Income Countries
authors: Shannon Barkley, Ernesto Bascolo, Hernan Montenegro, Shannon McManus
description: WHO Bulletin article identifying priorities for improving primary health care in LMICs, including community engagement and adapting PHC to evolving population needs for achieving UHC by 2030.

title: Guide Posts for Investment in Primary Health Care and Projected Resource Needs in 67 Low-Income and Middle-Income Countries: A Modelling Study
authors: Karin Stenberg, Odd Hanssen, Melanie Bertram, Tessa Tan-Torres Edejer, Agnese Ruscitti, Callum Brindley
description: Lancet Global Health modelling study estimating that an additional $200-328 billion per year is required for PHC from 2020-2030, with the greatest needs in low-income countries.

title: The World Health Report 2008: Primary Health Care - Now More Than Ever
description: Landmark WHO report revisiting primary health care 30 years after the Alma-Ata Declaration, advocating for universal access, equity, and social justice through four interlocking sets of PHC reforms.

title: Primary Health Care on the Road to Universal Health Coverage: 2019 Global Monitoring Report
description: WHO-World Bank monitoring report examining PHC service coverage indicators across income groups and regions, released ahead of the UN High-Level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage.

title: Delivering Non-Communicable Disease Services Through Primary Health Care in Selected South Asian Countries: Are Health Systems Prepared?
authors: Mehreen Riaz Hashmi, Syed Amjad Ali, Dina Balabanova, Sameen Siddiqi
description: Lancet Global Health Series paper examining NCD service delivery through PHC in South Asian countries, finding that implementation of PHC to address NCDs is currently insufficient despite rising NCD burden.

title: Self-Management of Chronic, Non-Communicable Diseases in South Asian Settings: A Systematic Mixed-Studies Review
authors: Farooq Siddiqui, Omara Dogar, Mona Kanaan, Kamran Siddiqi
description: Systematic review finding that age, education, income, knowledge, social support, self-efficacy, and affordability of medicine are key determinants of chronic NCD self-management in South Asia.

title: Leveraging Lessons from the COVID-19 Pandemic to Strengthen Low-Income and Middle-Income Country Preparedness for Future Global Health Threats
authors: Madhukar Pai, Nita Bhandari, Gagandeep Kang, Soumya Swaminathan
description: Lancet Infectious Diseases paper analyzing how the global COVID-19 response failed to fully prevent morbidity and mortality in LMICs and outlining lessons for future pandemic preparedness.

title: Pandemic Preparedness and COVID-19: An Exploratory Analysis of Infection and Fatality Rates, and Contextual Factors Associated with Preparedness in 177 Countries
description: Large-scale Lancet analysis of pandemic preparedness across 177 countries, finding that traditional preparedness indices like the Global Health Security Index failed to predict COVID-19 pandemic outcomes.

title: Global Investments in Pandemic Preparedness and COVID-19: Development Assistance and Domestic Spending on Health Between 1990 and 2026
authors: Global Burden of Disease Health Financing Collaborator Network
description: Lancet Global Health open access study highlighting financing gaps in national health systems, especially in LMICs, that contributed to pandemic unpreparedness.

title: Financing Pandemic Prevention, Preparedness and Response: Lessons Learned and Perspectives for Future
authors: Iyiola Olatunji, Olawale Oladejo, Oyewale Tomori
description: Globalization and Health paper arguing that global health security goals and UHC will remain unachievable unless African and developing country health systems are adequately funded for public health emergency resilience.

title: Social Determinants of Health: The Role of Effective Communication in the COVID-19 Pandemic in Developing Countries
description: Open access paper in Global Health Action arguing that effective crisis communication is among the most critical social determinants of health for reducing COVID-19 morbidity and relieving pressure on weak health systems in developing countries.

title: Social Determinants of Infectious Diseases in South Asia
authors: Bir Rawal, Rajesh Manandhar, Sameer Dixit
description: PMC open access paper examining social determinants of infectious diseases in South Asia with special reference to HIV and tuberculosis, concluding that addressing social factors is essential for sustainable intervention.

title: Social Determinants of Health, Report of a Regional Consultation, Colombo, Sri Lanka
authors: World Health Organization, Regional Office for South-East Asia
description: WHO South-East Asia regional consultation report on social determinants of health, examining health inequities driven by socioeconomic position and the double burden of disease in the region.

title: Towards Achievement of Universal Health Care in India by 2020: A Call to Action
authors: K Srinath Reddy, Vikram Patel, Prabhat Jha, Vinod K Paul, A K Shiva Kumar, Lalit Dandona
description: Landmark Lancet India Series paper arguing that sustaining India's economic trajectory requires giving the highest priority to universal health care, with a focus on equity, financial protection, and primary health care.

title: Health Care and Equity in India
authors: Vikram Patel, Rachana Parikh, Sunil Nandraj, Priya Balasubramaniam, Kavita Narayan, Vinod K Paul, A K Shiva Kumar, Mirai Chatterjee, K Srinath Reddy
description: Lancet India Series paper highlighting that more than three-quarters of health expenditure in India is out-of-pocket, with about 39 million people falling into poverty annually due to health spending.

title: Reimagining India's Health System: A Lancet Citizens' Commission
authors: K Srinath Reddy, Vikram Patel, Nachiket Mor, Srinivasan Ramani
description: Lancet Citizens' Commission laying out the path to achieving UHC in India, proposing a transformative approach that places citizen needs at the center of health system reform.

title: The Burden of Child and Maternal Malnutrition and Trends in Its Indicators in the States of India: The Global Burden of Disease Study 1990-2017
authors: India State-Level Disease Burden Initiative Malnutrition Collaborators
description: GBD study analyzing child and maternal malnutrition trends across Indian states, finding high prevalence of stunting in less-developed states and paradoxically high wasting in some more-developed states.

title: Demographic, Socioeconomic and Regional Disparities in the Coverage of Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Facilities in Four South Asian Countries
authors: Md Hasibul Islam Jitu, Mohammad Shahed Masud
description: PLOS ONE study using MICS data from Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Nepal, and Pakistan to examine WASH facility coverage disparities and identify significant socioeconomic and demographic factors.

title: Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (WASH) Factors and the Incidence of Communicable Diseases in Urban Bangladesh: Evidence from Municipal Areas
authors: Md Nuruzzaman Khan, Raaj Kishore Biswas, Shuva Das
description: PLOS NTDs study finding that nearly one in four participants in urban Bangladesh reported communicable diseases, with diarrhoea most common and poor hand hygiene showing the strongest disease association.

title: Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH): The Evolution of a Global Health and Development Sector
authors: Sara de Wit, Euphrasia Luseka, David Bradley, Joe Brown, Jayant Bhagwan, Barbara Evans, Matthew C Freeman, Guy Howard, Isha Ray, Ian Ross, Sheillah Simiyu, Oliver Cumming, Clare I R Chandler
description: BMJ Global Health open access article examining the evolution of the global WASH sector, noting that universal WASH access by 2030 under SDG 6 remains a distant prospect in many countries.

title: Progress on Household Drinking Water, Sanitation and Hygiene 2000-2020: Five Years into the SDGs
description: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme report presenting updated global and regional WASH estimates, serving as the leading source of comparable progress data for SDG 6 monitoring.

title: Essential Childhood Immunization in 43 Low- and Middle-Income Countries: Analysis of Spatial Trends and Socioeconomic Inequalities in Vaccine Coverage
authors: Anna Dimitrova, Jayanta Bhattacharya, Nirmala Naidoo, Ties Boerma
description: PLOS Medicine open access analysis of spatial trends and socioeconomic inequalities in essential childhood immunization coverage across 43 LMICs, revealing persistent wealth and geographic disparities.

title: Vaccine Equity in Low and Middle Income Countries: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
authors: Abimbola Olaniran, Aduragbemi Banke-Thomas, Helen Smith, Susan Tull
description: Systematic review highlighting that global DTP3 coverage stagnated at 85% since 2010, with 14 million infants missing initial doses in 2019, driven by wealth, education, gender, and geographic inequalities.

title: The 2024 Report of the Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change: Facing Record-Breaking Threats from Delayed Action
authors: Marina Romanello, Claudia di Napoli, Carole Green, Harry Kennard, Pete Lampard, Daniel Scamman, et al.
description: Annual Lancet Countdown report tracking 57 indicators on health and climate change, finding that heat exposure caused 470 billion lost labour hours globally in 2021, with losses equivalent to 5.6% of GDP in low-HDI countries.

title: Digital Health Interventions for Improving Access to Primary Care in India: A Scoping Review
authors: Smisha Agarwal, Yumna Vahanvaty, Manushi Patel, Olakunle Alonge
description: PLOS Global Public Health scoping review of digital health interventions to improve primary care access in India, examining telemedicine and mobile health approaches for vulnerable populations.

title: Monitoring Progress towards Universal Health Coverage at Country and Global Levels
authors: Margaret E Kruk, Anna D Gage, Catherine Arsenault, Keely Jordan, Hannah H Leslie, Sanam Roder-DeWan, Olusoji Adeyi, Pierre Barker, Bernadette Daelmans, Svetlana V Doubova
description: PLOS Medicine framework paper proposing methods for monitoring UHC progress at country and global levels, examining both service coverage and financial protection indicators.

title: Decentralization of Governance and Development
description: Examines the theoretical case for and against decentralization in developing and transition economies, analyzing how decentralization affects governance, accountability, and development outcomes in countries like China and India.

title: Decentralization and Service Delivery
authors: Junaid Ahmad, Shantayanan Devarajan, Stuti Khemani, Shekhar Shah
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper analyzing why basic services systematically fail the poor, finding that public spending has only a weak relationship with outcomes and arguing that improving service delivery is the key motivation behind decentralization efforts.

title: Decentralization in Developing Economies
description: Reviews the trade-offs of fiscal federalism in developing countries, explaining why developing nations have less decentralization than developed countries, particularly on the revenue side, and discussing the role of corruption, public goods, and taxation.

title: Implementing Decentralized Local Governance: A Treacherous Road with Potholes, Detours and Road Closures
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper examining the implementation of decentralized local governance across 74 countries during 1986-2001, analyzing how ethnic conflicts, fiscal crises, and political events drove reform agendas.

title: Decentralization, Governance, and Localizing SDGs in Asia and the Pacific
description: ADB publication examining decentralization and local governance reforms in Asia-Pacific, linking improvements in service delivery outcomes to national SDG priorities through localized governance frameworks.

title: Local Public Financial Management
description: World Bank volume covering public finance, intergovernmental relations, and local financial management in developing countries, with a focus on accountability in the public sector and infrastructure service provision.

title: World Development Report 2017: Governance and the Law
description: Explores why some policies fail and others succeed, arguing that effective policies must guarantee credible commitment, support coordination, and promote cooperation, while addressing power asymmetries that lead to exclusion, capture, and clientelism.

title: Corruption
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Rema Hanna, Sendhil Mullainathan
description: Provides a new framework for analyzing corruption in public bureaucracies, arguing that the standard moral hazard model must be extended to account for the reality that corruption often arises because the principals themselves may be corrupt or have limited enforcement capacity.

title: Corruption and Growth
description: Seminal paper analyzing subjective indices of corruption, red tape, and judicial efficiency across countries, finding that corruption lowers investment and thereby lowers economic growth, with results robust to controlling for endogeneity.

title: Exposing Corrupt Politicians: The Effects of Brazil's Publicly Released Audits on Electoral Outcomes
description: Examines whether access to information enhances political accountability using Brazil's anti-corruption audit program, finding that disclosure of corruption reduced incumbent re-election likelihood by approximately 20 percent, with effects amplified by local media presence.

title: Do Government Audits Reduce Corruption? Estimating the Impacts of Exposing Corrupt Politicians
authors: Eric Avis, Claudio Ferraz, Frederico Finan
description: Studies Brazil's anti-corruption program that randomly audits municipalities, finding that being audited in the past reduces future corruption by 8 percent and increases the likelihood of subsequent legal action by 20 percent.

title: Corruption in India: Bridging Research Evidence and Policy Options
description: Comprehensive review of empirical research on corruption in India identifying four key drivers: lack of enforcement capacity, regulatory complexity, lack of transparency, and societal norms, while examining policy reforms including technology-based solutions.

title: Corruption Dynamics: The Golden Goose Effect
description: Studies the effect of a statutory wage increase in India's NREGA employment guarantee scheme on corruption, finding large 'golden goose effects' where the prospect of future illicit rents reduced the total impact of the wage increase on theft by roughly 64 percent.

title: Obtaining a Driver's License in India: An Experimental Approach to Studying Corruption
authors: Marianne Bertrand, Simeon Djankov, Rema Hanna, Sendhil Mullainathan
description: Randomized experiment on driver's license allocation in India finding that 71% of license holders avoided the mandated driving test and corruption does not merely reflect transfers but actively distorts allocation of public services.

title: Enhancing Government Effectiveness and Transparency: The Fight Against Corruption
description: World Bank report drawing on collective expertise to develop enhanced anti-corruption strategies across public procurement, infrastructure, state-owned enterprises, customs administration, and service delivery sectors.

title: Local Capture: Evidence from a Central Government Transfer Program in Uganda
description: Uses panel data from Ugandan primary schools to show that schools received only 13% of education grants on average, with the bulk captured by local officials, demonstrating that actual education spending is regressive even when budget allocations are not.

title: Bureaucracy and Development
authors: Timothy J. Besley, Robin Burgess, Adnan Khan, Guo Xu
description: Reviews the connections between bureaucratic effectiveness and development, showing that bureaucratic quality across countries has been remarkably persistent and is strongly correlated with GDP per capita, while examining the building blocks of state capacity.

title: State Capacity and Economic Development: A Network Approach
authors: Daron Acemoglu, Camilo Garcia-Jimeno, James A. Robinson
description: Exploits historical variation in Colombia including colonial state presence and road networks to isolate the impact of state capacity on economic outcomes, demonstrating the importance of state capacity for long-run development.

title: The Origins of State Capacity: Property Rights, Taxation, and Politics
description: Develops a framework where policy choices are constrained by economic institutions reflecting past investments in legal and fiscal state capacity, showing how political stability and common interests drive investments in state capacity.

title: The Personnel Economics of the Developing State
authors: Frederico Finan, Benjamin A. Olken, Rohini Pande
description: Reviews field experiments on public sector personnel economics, examining how incentives, monitoring, and e-governance affect bureaucratic performance, noting that public sector employees in low-income countries enjoy the largest wage premiums over private sector counterparts.

title: Social Proximity and Bureaucrat Performance: Evidence from India
description: Finds that bureaucrats originating from the same home district as an incoming Chief Minister receive more transfers and promotions, with results suggesting social proximity adversely impacts bureaucratic performance through political favoritism.

title: Is India a Flailing State?: Detours on the Four Lane Highway to Modernization
description: Argues that India is a 'flailing state' where highly capable top-level institutions are disconnected from routine implementation, creating massive divergence between de jure and de facto reality in service delivery across education, health, and policing.

title: Building State Capability: Evidence, Analysis, Action
authors: Matt Andrews, Lant Pritchett, Michael Woolcock
description: Identifies capability traps including isomorphic mimicry and premature load bearing that hold governments back, and proposes Problem-Driven Iterative Adaptation (PDIA) as a process for building state capability in developing countries.

title: Traveling Agents: Political Change and Bureaucratic Turnover in India
description: Uses micro-level data on Indian IAS bureaucrats to show that politicians use frequent reassignments to control centrally recruited civil servants, with bureaucrat transfers significantly driven by changes in the Chief Minister's identity.

title: Missing in Action: Teacher and Health Worker Absence in Developing Countries
authors: Nazmul Chaudhury, Jeffrey Hammer, Michael Kremer, Karthik Muralidharan, F. Halsey Rogers
description: Reports results from unannounced visits to schools and clinics in six developing countries, finding average teacher absence of 19% and health worker absence of 35%, with absence rates higher in poorer regions.

title: Ghost Doctors: Absenteeism in Rural Bangladeshi Health Facilities
description: First nationally representative study of health worker absenteeism in Bangladesh finding a 35% absence rate overall and 74% for physicians at smaller subcenters, with absenteeism correlated with provider residence distance and opportunity cost of time.

title: Quality and Accountability in Healthcare Delivery: Audit-Study Evidence from Primary Care in India
authors: Jishnu Das, Alaka Holla, Aakash Mohpal, Karthik Muralidharan
description: Presents the first direct evidence on comparative quality of public and private healthcare in rural India using standardized patients, revealing significant gaps in provider quality and practice patterns.

title: Can Mandated Political Representation Increase Policy Influence for Disadvantaged Minorities? Theory and Evidence from India
description: Exploits political reservation features in Indian state legislatures to show that mandated representation for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes increases targeted transfers to those groups, suggesting incomplete policy commitment in democracies.

title: Pitfalls of Participatory Programs: Evidence from a Randomized Evaluation in Education in India
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Rukmini Banerji, Esther Duflo, Rachel Glennerster, Stuti Khemani
description: Evaluates three interventions to encourage community participation in Indian education, finding that information provision and training had no impact on community involvement or learning, while volunteer-led reading camps significantly improved reading skills.

title: The Political Economy of Public Expenditures
description: Background paper for WDR 2004 examining how political incentives shape public expenditure patterns, comparing Indian states like Kerala and Uttar Pradesh to illustrate how political institutions drive divergent development outcomes.

title: Making Politics Work for Development: Harnessing Transparency and Citizen Engagement
authors: Stuti Khemani, Ernesto Dal Bo, Claudio Ferraz, Frederico Finan, Corinne Stephenson, Adesinaola Odugbemi, Dikshya Thapa, Scott Abrahams
description: World Bank Policy Research Report arguing that transparency focused on political engagement is more effective than social accountability mechanisms alone, and that sustained reductions in corruption depend on fundamentally changing incentives in the public sector.

title: If Politics is the Problem, How Can External Actors Be Part of the Solution?
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper arguing that traditional development assistance bundling finance and knowledge into projects can perpetuate government failures, and proposing that knowledge should be targeted to strengthen political accountability.

title: Development Strategies: Integrating Governance and Growth
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper laying out four distinctive sequences through which governance, state capacity, and growth interact, including 'just enough governance' approaches that address specific institutional constraints only as they become binding.

title: Strengthening Public Financial Management: Exploring Drivers and Effects
description: Explores two relationships: between country characteristics and PFM quality (drivers), and between PFM quality and development outcomes, providing evidence on what drives improvements in public financial management systems.

title: Public Financial Management Reforms in Post-Conflict Countries: Synthesis Report
description: Synthesizes experiences of PFM reforms in post-conflict settings, arguing that a credible and controllable budget requires a minimal set of budget institutions and that developing countries with weak capacity face significant budget process challenges.

title: Conditional Cash Transfers: Reducing Present and Future Poverty
description: Comprehensive World Bank assessment of conditional cash transfer programs across developing countries, examining their effects on poverty reduction, education, health outcomes, and human capital accumulation.

title: Rethinking Social Protection in South Asia: Toward Progressive Universalism
authors: Rene Leon Solano, Jumana Alaref, Mark Dorfman, Zaineb Majoka, Mostafa Amir Sabbih, Elizabeth Mata Lorenzo
description: World Bank report arguing that South Asia has made significant progress in social protection delivery but still has far to go, advocating for progressive universalism as a framework for expanding coverage and improving adequacy.

title: Responsive by Design: Building Adaptive Social Protection Systems in South Asia
description: Provides a framework for adaptive social protection systems across four elements -- institutional arrangements, financing, programs, and data systems -- analyzing South Asian systems to guide policy dialogue on resilience.

title: Exploring Universal Basic Income: A Guide to Navigating Concepts, Evidence, and Practices
authors: Ugo Gentilini, Margaret Grosh, Jamele Rigolini, Ruslan Yemtsov (editors)
description: World Bank volume providing a framework for understanding UBI in different contexts, examining how it compares to targeted social assistance in terms of coverage, adequacy, incentives, poverty effects, and implementation feasibility.

title: State of Social Protection Report 2025: The 2-Billion-Person Challenge
description: Documents that 2 billion people in low- and middle-income countries remain uncovered by social protection, with three out of four people in low-income countries receiving no support and current growth rates requiring 18 years to achieve full coverage.

title: General Equilibrium Effects of (Improving) Public Employment Programs: Experimental Evidence from India
authors: Karthik Muralidharan, Paul Niehaus, Sandip Sukhtankar
description: Estimates direct and indirect effects of improving India's NREGS implementation through randomized rollout, finding that the reform raised beneficiary household earnings by 14%, reduced poverty by 26%, with 86% of income gains from higher private-sector wages.

title: Improving Last-Mile Service Delivery using Phone-Based Monitoring
authors: Karthik Muralidharan, Paul Niehaus, Sandip Sukhtankar, Sheetal Weaver
description: Large-scale experiment evaluating phone-based monitoring of a program transferring nearly a billion dollars to 5.7 million Indian farmers, finding a 7.6% reduction in non-delivery at a cost of only 3.6 cents per additional dollar delivered.

title: Do Lenders Favor Politically Connected Firms? Rent Provision in an Emerging Financial Market
description: Uses loan-level data covering over 90,000 firms in Pakistan to show that politically connected firms borrow 45% more and have 50% higher default rates, with preferential treatment occurring exclusively in government banks at an estimated annual cost of 0.3-1.9% of GDP.

title: Grappling with Aadhaar: Biometrics, Social Identity and the Indian State
description: Open-access special issue examining India's Aadhaar biometric identification program as part of a broader sociotechnical environment that deeply influences public governance, citizen-state relations, and service delivery including the Public Distribution System.

title: From Delay to Payday: Using a Digital Platform to Improve State Capacity for Welfare Payment Processing
authors: Karthik Muralidharan, Vaidyanathan Nandakumar, Gaurav Siddharth Sawant
description: Studies a digital dashboard platform for real-time tracking of NREGS payment processing in two Indian states, finding that access to the platform expedited bureaucrat payment processing by 17% and increased available worksites by 23%.

title: Why Nations Fail: The Origins of Power, Prosperity, and Poverty
description: Argues that nations thrive when they develop inclusive political and economic institutions that share power widely, and fail under extractive institutions that concentrate power among elites, drawing on examples from North and South Korea, Nogales, and colonial histories.

title: An Uncertain Glory: India and Its Contradictions
